untranslatable_reason: BARR (Bearish Arc Reversal) is a visual chart pattern requiring curved trend line detection and bump identification. This requires pattern recognition logic not expressible with the allowed indicators and operators. The signal specifies: (1) curved trend line with reversal characteristics - requires trend fitting algorithm; (2) dual/multiple bumps - requires peak detection; (3) second bump peaks above first only 8% of the time - requires peak comparison logic. No combination of price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, or Volume indicators can reliably identify the formation and bumps of a BARR pattern. Author also marked stop placement and target computation as AUTHOR_SILENT, leaving exit strategy undefined.
translation_notes: Placeholder entry/exit conditions use pivot crossover as a generic breakout proxy, but this does not capture the true BARR pattern mechanics. Requires custom pattern recognition module or manual chart review for accurate signal generation.
